subject: [RFC PATCH 27/45] mm: trigger deferred SPB evacuation when atomic allocs would taint a clean SPB

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory allocation system to trigger deferred superpage block (SPB) evacuation when an atomic allocation would taint a clean SPB. The patch introduces new data structures and functions to manage deferred SPB evacuations, which are scheduled on the zone's workqueue. This approach aims to reduce the number of clean SPBs being tainted by atomic allocations, allowing subsequent allocations to claim pages from tainted pools instead. The trigger is gated by prechecks to prevent storming the workqueue.
